G 18 RegistryOIML Term-Usage Registry
ActionsTermsTC / SCPublicationsEditionsHarmoniseID ConflictsDivergence
Registry / ID Conflicts

Duplicate & conflicting G 18 IDs

Two types of ID issues across editions: raw conflicts (one ID assigned to two different concepts — a numbering error in the source publication) and designation collisions (the same term cited under multiple distinct IDs — the harmonisation worklist for TC 1).

Raw ID conflicts (same ID for different concepts)

The source publication used the same G 18 number for two semantically different concepts. In 2010 these are resolved via the <id>a/<id>b split convention.

2010 (7 conflicting IDs)

IDDistinct concepts sharing the ID
00474
flow sensor or volume sensor from OIML R049-2:2006 (00474a)
rated operating conditions from OIML R060:2000 (00474b)
00763
load receptor from OIML R061-1:2004 (00763a)
weighing unit from OIML R061-1:2004 (00763b)
01213
measuring system from OIML R105:1993 (01213a)
total train from OIML R106-1:1997 (01213b)
01291
analogue indication from OIML R106-1:1997 (01291a)
durability from OIML R106-1:1997 (01291b)
01508
automatic refractometer from OIML R108:1993 (01508a)
indicating unit from OIML R114:1995 (01508b)
01669
measuring mode from OIML R126:1998 (01669a)
test procedure from OIML R125:1998 (01669b)
02348
conventional true quantity value from OIML R099-1:2008 (02348a)
warm-up time from OIML R099-1:2008 (02348b)

Designation collisions (same concept, multiple IDs)

The same term name appears under multiple distinct G 18 IDs because each OIML publication that cites the term gets its own entry. TC 1 must decide which ID becomes canonical in the 202X harmonisation.

Summary by edition

Metric2010202X
Designations with multiple IDs285392
Total IDs participating in duplication12211667
Designations with exactly 2 IDs134190
Designations with ≥ 3 IDs151202
Designations with ≥ 5 IDs64100
Designations with ≥ 10 IDs2323

audit trail8801395, 02061, 02458, 02599, 02872…
automatic zero-setting device8800922, 01336, 01896, 02563, 02827-R050-1…
device8800344, 02217, 02623, 02978, 02979…
indicator8800586, 00898, 01343, 01622, 02548…

What this means for TC 1

  • Raw conflicts (7 in 2010, 0 in 202X): numbering errors in the source publication — already resolved via a/b splits. 202X is clean.
  • Designation collisions (284 in 2010, 391 in 202X): the same term appears under many IDs because each OIML publication that cites it gets a separate G 18 entry. TC 1's harmonisation task is to decide, for each duplicated designation, whether to:
    • Merge: collapse all instances into a single canonical definition in 202X.
    • Keep separate: document why each publication uses a deliberately different definition.
  • The harmonisation worklist shows every term with divergent definitions for side-by-side comparison.
G 18 — OIML Term-Usage Registry
Source: oimlsmart/vocab. VIM/VIML: oimlsmart.github.io/vocab.
